Success is achieved when a pushable object is correctly positioned on the goal tile(s), satisfying the WIN condition.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement of the selected piece was possible by either clicking a position (teleporting the piece to the chosen location) or by using directional move actions (MOVE_LEFT, MOVE_RIGHT, etc.), which shifted the piece by discrete steps. Clicking on a tile moved the selected piece there instantly, whereas MOVE actions would move the piece in the specified direction if not blocked by a wall or an obstacle. If the path was blocked, the piece could not move in that direction. Step count was limited per level, and efficient planning was required to reach the goal within that budget. Only one piece could be actively controlled/selected and moved at a time.
